---
title: "Project_2_Covid19_Cases_Data"
author: "Khandker Qaiduzzaman"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Objective

Transform the wide-format global COVID-19 confirmed cases dataset into a tidy (long) format using the `tidyr` and `dplyr` packages in R. Once the data has been cleaned up, examine how confirmed COVID-19 cases have evolved over time across nations to find broad global patterns.

# Approach

### Dataset description

For this assignment, I will use a global COVID-19 confirmed cases dataset provided by the Johns Hopkins University Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE). The dataset is publicly available and can be accessed here:

<https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NafeesKhandker/Project-2-Tidy-Format-Transformation/refs/heads/main/Covid19_confirmed_global.csv>

Beginning in January 2020, the dataset includes daily confirmed COVID-19 case counts for various nations and regions worldwide.

At the moment, the dataset is kept in a wide format in which a particular date is represented by each column and a country or region by each row. For instance, the number of confirmed cases reported on those dates is shown in columns like `1/22/20`, `1/23/20`, and `1/24/20`.

The dataset does not adhere to tidy data principles because the date variable is embedded within column headers rather than being stored as a separate variable. Every variable in tidy data should have its own column, and every observation should have its own row.

The following significant variables are included in the dataset:

-   **Province/State**: Sub-national region
-   **Country/Region**: Country name
-   **Lat**: Latitude coordinate
-   **Long**: Longitude coordinate
-   **Date columns**: Daily confirmed case counts

### Anticipated Challenges

One anticipated challenge is handling the extremely large number of date columns in the dataset. These columns span multiple years and must be transformed into a single date variable during the tidying process.

Another potential challenge is the presence of missing values in the Province/State column, since not all countries report regional data. Care must be taken to preserve country information while handling these missing values appropriately.

Additionally, date values stored as column names will need to be converted into proper date formats after the reshaping process.

### Implementation of Data Import

```{r}
library(tidyverse) 
library(gt)

url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NafeesKhandker/Project-2-Tidy-Format-Transformation/refs/heads/main/Covid19_confirmed_global.csv"

df <- read_csv(file = url, show_col_types = FALSE)

df |> gt()
```
